items.title: Carney to announce affordability measures after securing majority in byelection wins
items.summary_auto: A day after sweeping three byelections in Ontario and Quebec that gave him a majority in the House of Commons, Prime Minister Mark Carney will try to give voters a reason to be reassured with the rising cost of living Tuesday by announcing new affordability measures.

items.title: More talks urged for U.S., Iran as shipping standoff continues
items.summary_auto: The standoff between the United States and Iran deepened on Tuesday as the U.S. declared it had blockaded Iran's ports, Tehran threatened to strike targets across the region and Pakistan said it was racing to bring the sides together for more talks.

items.title: Alberta rejects federal nature strategy, redefines protected land
items.summary_auto: Ottawa has a plan to protect 30 per cent of Canadian land by 2030, but Alberta has rejected the federal benchmarks. The province claims it already protects 60 per cent of its land. This comes after it has expanded the definition of protected lands in a move a conservationist calls concerning.

items.title: Director of Camp Mystic says he slept through warning of deadly Texas floods
items.summary_auto: The director of the Texas summer camp where 27 campers and counsellors were killed by a devastating flood last year testified Monday he did not see official warnings issued the day before the storm hit and that staff had no meetings about the pending danger.

items.title: B.C. declared toxic drugs a public health emergency 10 years ago. Has it made a difference?
items.summary_auto: Experts say they worry the province hasn’t used all the powers available that come with a public health emergency, and is leaning on public opinion rather than science to inform policy.

items.title: At least 16 wounded, gunman dead in school shooting in Turkey
items.summary_auto: A former student opened fire at a high school in southeast Turkey on Tuesday, wounding at least 16 people before killing himself, officials said.

items.title: 'Gone through life together': How a rural N.S. book club became a support network
items.summary_auto: After their son's death in 2005, Dan and Jacinta Harvey of Berwick, N.S., began opening their doors to friends and casual acquaintances alike to discuss books and life.

items.title: N.L. watchdog flagged 'potential billing fraud' for travel nurse EV rentals. We have the invoices
items.summary_auto: A critical report by Newfoundland and Labrador's auditor general highlighted electric vehicle rentals charged to taxpayers by a travel nursing agency. Now, a CBC Investigates analysis of billing records obtained through access to information also raises some questions.

items.title: CBC investigation finds grocers Loblaw, Sobeys overcharging for underweight meat — again
Israel and Lebanon are set for historic talks. But almost no one believes they'll succeed
items.summary_auto: Rare, bilateral talks between senior Lebanese and Israeli officials set for Tuesday in Washington could pave the way for a ceasefire — but almost no one appears to believe that will happen. There is dismay and a feeling of hopelessness across Lebanon as it reels from the aftermath of Israel’s ferocious attack on Wednesday that killed over 300 people.

items.title: Canadian Coast Guard tests Arctic surveillance drone as air force delivery stalls
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Coast Guard will deploy its new Hermes 900 Starliner drone for Arctic test flights this summer after a federal restructuring shifted surveillance assets to Department of National Defence. With MQ-9B SkyGuardian drones delayed until 2028, civilian operators gain an unexpected operational edge in the Far North.

items.title: Domestic assault trial for Mike Strange, Ontario councillor and ex-Olympian, set to begin today
items.summary_auto: Niagara Falls, Ont., city Coun. Mike Strange is on trial for domestic assault, with proceedings in St. Catharines set to begin today. Strange, first elected to council in 2014 after an Olympic boxing career, has maintained his innocence and has remained in his jhob since being charged in May.

items.title: 6 weeks into war, Quebec's Iranian diaspora shares feelings of fear, relief and resolve
items.summary_auto: When Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali  Khamenei was killed in a Feb. 28 attack by the U.S. and Israel, hundreds of Iranian Quebecers rejoiced in the streets. Six weeks later, the conflict between the United States and Israel with Iran is drawing mixed response from the diaspora.

items.title: These U.S. companies think Trump's tariffs are great. Here's why
items.summary_auto: The White House is on a push to showcase the success stories of U.S. manufacturers who are benefitting from tariffs, one that saw U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, a member of Trump's cabinet, setting out last week on a two-day tour of factories in Ohio and Michigan.

items.title: #TheMoment a shoe cobbler got a new sewing machine from the community
items.summary_auto: Toronto shoe cobbler Alazar Beyene and one of his customers, Marsha Shandur, tell The National about the moment the community raised more than $11,000 to purchase a new sewing machine for his business — Moneysworth & Best Quality Shoe Repair in the Dufferin Mall.

items.title: Queen Latifah, Billy Idol, Sade and Oasis among artists being inducted into Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
items.summary_auto: Phil Collins, Iron Maiden, Billy Idol, Queen Latifah, Oasis, Sade and Joy Division/New Order will be inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, along with first-time nominees Wu-Tang Clan and the late Luther Vandross.

items.title: Mark Carney enters his majority era
items.summary_auto: There have been a total of 24 prime ministers in Canada's history, but before Monday night only 13 could claim to have led their party to a majority of seats in the House of Commons. Mark Carney is now the 14th to do it.

items.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz blockade hasn't tanked markets — yet
items.summary_auto: With the Strait of Hormuz now doubly blockaded, oil prices fell while markets climbed on Monday, despite an initial shock. CBC senior business correspondent Peter Armstrong explains where the pain is being felt, and how public faith in a solution could be buoying the markets.

items.title: Man accused of attacking Sam Altman's home charged with 2 counts of attempted murder
items.summary_auto: The man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home has been charged with attempting to kill Altman and a security guard at the residence, San Francisco district attorney Brooke Jenkins said Monday.

items.title: Toronto Tempo select UCLA guard Kiki Rice with 1st-ever WNBA draft pick
items.summary_auto: Kiki Rice is the Toronto Tempo's first-ever WNBA draft pick. The Tempo selected the UCLA guard sixth overall in the first round Monday, 10 days after adding its first players in the WNBA expansion draft.

items.title: How will the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports work, and what will it achieve?
items.summary_auto: The U.S. military has initiated a blockade across the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea, a move experts say is an act of war with potential for serious escalation in the midst of a ceasefire. U.S. Central Command says it is targeting all vessels traveling to and from Iran as of Monday morning

items.title: Advocates, government make final arguments in B.C. MAID trial
items.summary_auto: Closing arguments started Monday in a trial that will determine whether faith-based health care facilities can continue to prohibit medical assistance in dying in B.C.

items.title: Quebec’s next premier outlines priorities during 1st day on the job
items.summary_auto: Premier-designate Christine Fréchette held her first news conference at the National Assembly, announcing she’s focused on addressing rising expenses like housing and groceries in the province. With five months until the provincial election, the new CAQ leader doesn’t have a lot of time to make her mark with voters.

items.title: Why President Trump and Pope Leo are feuding | About That
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump took shots at Pope Leo XIV on Truth Social after the pontiff criticized Washington's role in the war in the Middle East. Andrew Chang explains how Trump's issues with Pope Leo are reaching a new public boiling point. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock and Getty Images

items.title: Hootsuite founder Ryan Holmes returns to CEO role on interim basis, as Irina Novoselsky exits
items.summary_auto: The CEO of Hootsuite has stepped down after spending the last several months defending a contract between the company and a controversial U.S. government department, making way for the firm's founder to return on an interim basis.

items.title: Canadian women pick up beach volleyball silver in their Pro Tour season opener
items.summary_auto: Canada's Melissa Humana-Paredes and Brandie Wilkerson opened a new beach volleyball season earning a silver medal at the Saquarema Elite tournament in Brazil as Taryn Brasher and Kristen Cruz of the United States prevailed 21-15, 21-15 on Sunday.

items.title: Orbán’s defeat may signal shift in Europe’s far-right, illiberal tide says Michael Ignatieff
items.summary_auto: For Michael Ignatieff, news that Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule in Hungary had come to an end was reason enough to raise a glass. He speaks about what Orbán’s defeat and Péter Magyar's victory could mean for geopolitics.

items.title: Guns, jewels and a heist: Inside the fake world police built for accused killer Dean Penney
items.summary_auto: The RCMP conducted a four-year undercover investigation aimed at getting Dean Penney to open up about his estranged wife's disappearance. He moved weapons, diamonds, and even participated in a fuel heist at the U.S. border. It was all fake.

items.title: A single vote, a Supreme Court decision: Why this riding is voting again
items.summary_auto: Terrebonne is one of three federal ridings holding a byelection on Monday. But unlike the two ridings in Toronto, this byelection isn’t over a vacant seat.

items.title: Port of Montreal leadership jumps ship as CFO follows CEO out the door
items.summary_auto: Barely a week after the abrupt departure of its CEO, the Montreal Port Authority confirmed its chief financial officer is leaving as well, part of an exodus that raises questions about the direction and governance of the country's second-largest port — and Ottawa is seeking answers.

items.title: Gap between richest and poorest Canadians widened again in 2025, StatsCan says
items.summary_auto: Statistics Canada says the income gap — measuring the difference in the share of disposable income between households in the top 40 per cent and those in the bottom 40 per cent — reached 46.7 percentage points in 2025.

items.title: Texas attorney general launches investigation into Lululemon's potential use of certain chemicals
items.summary_auto: The investigation, announced on the attorney general's website Monday, concerns whether Lululemon's athletic apparel contains PFAS, or perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances.

items.title: Ruby Rose accuses Katy Perry of sexual assault, which singer denies
items.summary_auto: Australian actress Ruby Rose alleged in a post on social media that American singer Katy Perry sexually assaulted her at a nightclub 20 years ago, a claim the pop star's representatives deny. The allegation first came in response to a Complex post on Instagram about Perry’s reaction to singer Justin Bieber’s headlining Coachella set.

items.title: Alberta moves to allow private diagnostic tests without a doctor’s referral
items.summary_auto: Bill 29, if passed, would allow Albertans to self-refer to private clinics across the province, later this year.

items.title: Minnesota authorities probe ICE arrest of St. Paul man as possible kidnapping
items.summary_auto: A Minnesota county is investigating the arrest of a Hmong American man by federal officers that was captured on video as a potential case of kidnapping, burglary and false imprisonment, officials announced Monday.

items.title: Hollywood stars express opposition to Paramount-Warner merger in open letter
items.summary_auto: More than a thousand movie stars, writers, directors and other Hollywood professionals announced their "unequivocal opposition" to the proposed Paramount merger with Warner Bros. Discovery in an open letter published Monday.

items.title: Spot solid science and avoid fake claims
items.summary_auto: Science is under attack. In an era of AI, instant misinformation and hidden agendas, how do you tell the difference between real science and well-packaged fraud? CBC News's Johanna Wagstaffe breaks down the peer review process — the critical filter that determines what science the public can trust.

items.title: Florida teen charged with aggravated sexual abuse, murder of stepsister on cruise ship
items.summary_auto: A 16-year-old boy has been charged with murder and aggravated sexual abuse in Florida in the death of his 18-year-old stepsister on a Carnival Cruise Line ship, the U.S. Justice Department said Monday.

items.title: Trump administration reverses ban on flying Pride flag at Stonewall monument
items.summary_auto: The Trump administration said Monday it will resume flying a rainbow Pride flag on a federal flagpole at the Stonewall National Monument in New York City, reversing course after removing the banner in February.

items.title: 2 central N.L. shootings in 2 days reignite calls for more resources to protect vulnerable women, children
items.summary_auto: People in Central Newfoundland are trying to make sense of a stunning cascade of tragedies and deadly shootings in recent days. And it’s again raising questions about how to address the systemic problem of violence against women.

items.title: Iranians try to see through fog of war amid internet blackout
items.summary_auto: Iranian authorities launched a full internet blackout during protests in January and the brutal repression that ended them. This was lifted somewhat in the weeks that followed, only to be reimposed in full when the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran kicked off on Feb. 28.

items.title: Super Mario Galaxy, Project Hail Mary help support best box office since start of pandemic
items.summary_auto: A string of box office successes is, perhaps surprisingly, supported by what seemed a fading remnant of the Hollywood landscape just a few years ago: non-franchises and even the occasional original feature.

items.title: Judge throws out Trump's $10B US lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein birthday card
items.summary_auto: A federal judge on Monday dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump’s defamation lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal, a setback for Trump in his legal campaign against media companies he accuses of treating him unfairly.

items.title: Ontario capping number of school trustees per board, creating new oversight roles
